title: Governance + community co-design protocol + reviewer roster
context: open-accessible-fonts involves community co-design for under-served scripts and lived-experience review for accessibility work. Without a published governance protocol and reviewer roster (even with roles TBD), there is no agreed process for community veto, change requests, or reviewer assignment. Depends on fonts-legal-003 to have the provenance and license framework in place first.
objective: Write and publish the project governance document, community co-design protocol (including community veto and change-request rights for script partners), and a reviewer roster template (roles defined even if names are TBD) covering maintainer, type design reviewer, script authority/community partner, accessibility specialist, lived-experience reviewers, and steward.
acceptanceCriteria: Governance document defines project roles: maintainer, type design reviewer, script authority/community partner, accessibility/vision specialist, lived-experience reviewer(s), steward, requestor/beneficiary.
Community co-design protocol states that script partners hold an effective veto on glyph forms and naming, and that decisions and change-requests are logged publicly.
Protocol specifies the entry gate for M2: a named community partner must co-sign the orthography and design brief before any glyph drawing begins.
Reviewer roster template is included with role definitions and placeholder slots for TBD names; consent-only credit and pseudonym policy stated.
Release checklist references reviewer sign-off requirements from PLAN.md section 8 (QA/review gates).
Document is CC-BY-4.0; no identifiable personal data included without consent.
output: A governance document (CC-BY-4.0) defining roles, co-design protocol with community veto rights, reviewer roster template, and release checklist, merged into the repository.
